figures.caption: Table 1. Last task accuracy (AT ), average accuracy (Ā), and weighted average accuracy (wĀ) on VFN186-LT, VFN186-Insulin, VFN186T2D, and Food101-LT with ρ = 0.01. Results are averaged over 5 runs. The best results are in bold and the second-best results are underlined.

Figure 3. Performance comparison under different step-imbalance ratios ρ on VFN186-LT and Food101-LT with T = 10 incremental steps. Our method consistently achieves higher and more stable performance across tasks under all imbalance settings.

Figure 2. Overview of DIME. (a) Sequential food datasets are learned with lightweight adapters on a frozen backbone using Balanced Softmax. After each step, the new adapter is merged with the accumulated base adapter to produce a unified model. (b) The merging module performs spectral alignment via SVD, class-count guided weighting, and rank-wise threshold modulation.
